mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.83% as of 2024-10-01 reflects recent rate cuts from higher levels
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2024-10-01 represents current labor market tightness
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in real GDP growth and retail sales signals weakening aggregate demand. When both GDPC1 growth and RSAFS decline simultaneously, equity valuations compress due to reduced earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 1.5% annualized AND RSAFS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23478.57B and RSAFS at 708290M as of 2024-10-01
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (GDPC1 × RSAFS) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production over 2 months signals supply-side weakness and reduced corporate profitability. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.4695 (revised) as of 2024-10-01 shows recent weakness
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window when INDPRO declines consecutively.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of aggregate demand. When HOUST rises above its 6-month moving average, it signals strengthening construction activity and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in less than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE) signals a sharp labor market shock. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice recession risk and corporate earnings.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50K month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 157945K (revised) and UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2024-10-01
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in less than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4%, the real policy rate is negative, signaling monetary accommodation. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real asset valuations benefit from negative real rates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 315.631 and FEDFUNDS at 4.83% as of 2024-10-01
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL - FEDFUNDS) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) serves as a continuous signal of economic momentum. Higher quarterly GDP growth rates produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprice corporate earnings expectations upward in response to stronger macroeconomic fundamentals.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23478.57B (revised) as of 2024-07-01 reflects recent economic activity
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 55% of periods when GDPC1 growth is above trend (> 2.5% annualized), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
